{ SandboxState, SandboxApi, SandboxBackupStateEnum, Configuration } from '@daytona/api-client';\r\nimport type { Sandbox as SandboxDto, SandboxListItem as SandboxListItemDto, PortPreviewUrl, SandboxVolume, BuildInfo, SshAccessDto, SshAccessValidationDto, SignedPortPreviewUrl, UpdateSandboxNetworkSettings, SandboxListSortField, SandboxListSortDirection } from '@daytona/api-client';\r\nimport type { Resources } from './Daytona';\r\nimport { FileSystem } from './FileSystem';\r\nimport { Git } from './Git';\r\nimport { Process } from './Process';\r\nimport { LspLanguageId, LspServer } from './LspServer';\r\nimport { ComputerUse } from './ComputerUse';\r\nimport type { AxiosInstance } from 'axios';\r\nimport { CodeInterpreter } from './CodeInterpreter';\r\n/**\r\n * Represents a Daytona Sandbox.\r\n *\r\n * @property {FileSystem} fs - File system operations interface\r\n * @property {Git} git - Git operations interface\r\n * @property {Process} process - Process execution interface\r\n * @property {CodeInterpreter} codeInterpreter - Stateful interpreter interface for executing code.\r\n *   Currently supports only Python. For other languages, use the `process.codeRun` method.\r\n * @property {ComputerUse} computerUse - Computer use operations interface for desktop automation\r\n * @property {string} id - Unique identifier for the Sandbox\r\n * @property {string} organizationId - Organization ID of the Sandbox\r\n * @property {string} [snapshot] - Daytona snapshot used to create the Sandbox\r\n * @property {string} user - OS user running in the Sandbox\r\n * @property {Record<string, string>} [env] - Environment variables set in the Sandbox\r\n * (not returned by list results; call `refreshData()` on each item to populate)\r\n * @property {Record<string, string>} labels - Custom labels attached to the Sandbox\r\n * @property {boolean} public - Whether the Sandbox is publicly accessible\r\n * @property {string} target - Target location of the runner where the Sandbox runs\r\n * @property {number} cpu - Number of CPUs allocated to the Sandbox\r\n * @property {number} gpu - Number of GPUs allocated to the Sandbox\r\n * @property {number} memory - Amount of memory allocated to the Sandbox in GiB\r\n * @property {number} disk - Amount of disk space allocated to the Sandbox in GiB\r\n * @property {SandboxState} state - Current state of the Sandbox (e.g., \"started\", \"stopped\")\r\n * @property {string} [errorReason] - Error message if Sandbox is in error state\r\n * @property {boolean} [recoverable] - Whether the Sandbox error is recoverable.\r\n * @property {SandboxBackupStateEnum} [backupState] - Current state of Sandbox backup\r\n * @property {string} [backupCreatedAt] - When the backup was created (not returned by list results;\r\n * call `refreshData()` on each item to populate)\r\n * @property {number} [autoStopInterval] - Auto-stop interval in minutes\r\n * @property {number} [autoArchiveInterval] - Auto-archive interval in minutes\r\n * @property {number} [autoDeleteInterval] - Auto-delete interval in minutes\r\n * @property {Array<SandboxVolume>} [volumes] - Volumes attached to the Sandbox (not returned by\r\n * list results; call `refreshData()` on each item to populate)\r\n * @property {BuildInfo} [buildInfo] - Build information for the Sandbox if it was created from dynamic build\r\n * (not returned by list results; call `refreshData()` on each item to populate)\r\n * @property {string} [createdAt] - When the Sandbox was created\r\n * @property {string} [updatedAt] - When the Sandbox was last updated\r\n * @property {string} [lastActivityAt] - When the Sandbox last had activity\r\n * @property {boolean} [networkBlockAll] - Whether to block all network access for the Sandbox\r\n * (not returned by list results; call `refreshData()` on each item to populate)\r\n * @property {string} [networkAllowList] - Comma-separated list of allowed CIDR network addresses for the Sandbox\r\n * (not returned by list results; call `refreshData()` on each item to populate)\r\n *\r\n * @class\r\n */\r\nexport declare class Sandbox {\r\n    private readonly clientConfig;\r\n    private readonly axiosInstance;\r\n    private readonly sandboxApi;\r\n    readonly fs: FileSystem;\r\n    readonly git: Git;\r\n    readonly process: Process;\r\n    readonly computerUse: ComputerUse;\r\n    readonly codeInterpreter: CodeInterpreter;\r\n    id: string;\r\n    name: string;\r\n    organizationId: string;\r\n    snapshot?: string;\r\n    user: string;\r\n    env?: Record<string, string>;\r\n    labels: Record<string, string>;\r\n    public: boolean;\r\n    target: string;\r\n    cpu: number;\r\n    gpu: number;\r\n    memory: number;\r\n    disk: number;\r\n    state?: SandboxState;\r\n    errorReason?: string;\r\n    recoverable?: boolean;\r\n    backupState?: SandboxBackupStateEnum;\r\n    backupCreatedAt?: string;\r\n    autoStopInterval?: number;\r\n    autoArchiveInterval?: number;\r\n    autoDeleteInterval?: number;\r\n    volumes?: Array<SandboxVolume>;\r\n    buildInfo?: BuildInfo;\r\n    createdAt?: string;\r\n    updatedAt?: string;\r\n    lastActivityAt?: string;\r\n    networkBlockAll?: boolean;\r\n    networkAllowList?: string;\r\n    toolboxProxyUrl: string;\r\n    private infoApi;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Creates a new Sandbox instance\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {SandboxDto} sandboxDto - The API Sandbox instance\r\n     */\r\n    constructor(sandboxDto: SandboxDto | SandboxListItemDto, clientConfig: Configuration, axiosInstance: AxiosInstance, sandboxApi: SandboxApi);\r\n    /**\r\n     * Gets the user's home directory path for the logged in user inside the Sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * @returns {Promise<string | undefined>} The absolute path to the Sandbox user's home directory for the logged in user\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const userHomeDir = await sandbox.getUserHomeDir();\r\n     * console.log(`Sandbox user home: ${userHomeDir}`);\r\n     */\r\n    getUserHomeDir(): Promise<string | undefined>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * @deprecated Use `getUserHomeDir` instead. This method will be removed in a future version.\r\n     */\r\n    getUserRootDir(): Promise<string | undefined>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Gets the working directory path inside the Sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * @returns {Promise<string | undefined>} The absolute path to the Sandbox working directory. Uses the WORKDIR specified\r\n     * in the Dockerfile if present, or falling back to the user's home directory if not.\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const workDir = await sandbox.getWorkDir();\r\n     * console.log(`Sandbox working directory: ${workDir}`);\r\n     */\r\n    getWorkDir(): Promise<string | undefined>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Creates a new Language Server Protocol (LSP) server instance.\r\n     *\r\n     * The LSP server provides language-specific features like code completion,\r\n     * diagnostics, and more.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {LspLanguageId} languageId - The language server type (e.g., \"typescript\")\r\n     * @param {string} pathToProject - Path to the project root directory. Relative paths are resolved based on the sandbox working directory.\r\n     * @returns {LspServer} A new LSP server instance configured for the specified language\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const lsp = await sandbox.createLspServer('typescript', 'workspace/project');\r\n     */\r\n    createLspServer(languageId: LspLanguageId | string, pathToProject: string): Promise<LspServer>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Sets labels for the Sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * Labels are key-value pairs that can be used to organize and identify Sandboxes.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {Record<string, string>} labels - Dictionary of key-value pairs representing Sandbox labels\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Set sandbox labels\r\n     * await sandbox.setLabels({\r\n     *   project: 'my-project',\r\n     *   environment: 'development',\r\n     *   team: 'backend'\r\n     * });\r\n     */\r\n    setLabels(labels: Record<string, string>): Promise<Record<string, string>>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Start the Sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * This method starts the Sandbox and waits for it to be ready.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {number} [timeout] - Maximum time to wait in seconds. 0 means no timeout.\r\n     *                            Defaults to 60-second timeout.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     * @throws {DaytonaError} - `DaytonaError` - If Sandbox fails to start or times out\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const sandbox = await daytona.getCurrentSandbox('my-sandbox');\r\n     * await sandbox.start(40);  // Wait up to 40 seconds\r\n     * console.log('Sandbox started successfully');\r\n     */\r\n    start(timeout?: number): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Recover the Sandbox from a recoverable error and wait for it to be ready.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {number} [timeout] - Maximum time to wait in seconds. 0 means no timeout.\r\n     *                            Defaults to 60-second timeout.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     * @throws {DaytonaError} - `DaytonaError` - If Sandbox fails to recover or times out\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const sandbox = await daytona.get('my-sandbox-id');\r\n     * await sandbox.recover();\r\n     * console.log('Sandbox recovered successfully');\r\n     */\r\n    recover(timeout?: number): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Stops the Sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * This method stops the Sandbox and waits for it to be fully stopped.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {number} [timeout] - Maximum time to wait in seconds. 0 means no timeout.\r\n     *                            Defaults to 60-second timeout.\r\n     * @param {boolean} [force] - If true, uses SIGKILL instead of SIGTERM. Defaults to false.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const sandbox = await daytona.get('my-sandbox-id');\r\n     * await sandbox.stop();\r\n     * console.log('Sandbox stopped successfully');\r\n     */\r\n    stop(timeout?: number, force?: boolean): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Forks the Sandbox, creating a new Sandbox with an identical filesystem.\r\n     *\r\n     * The forked Sandbox is a copy-on-write clone of the original. It starts\r\n     * with the same disk contents but operates independently from that point on.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {object} [params] - Fork parameters\r\n     * @param {string} [params.name] - Optional name for the forked Sandbox. If not provided, a unique name will be generated.\r\n     * @param {number} [timeout] - Maximum time to wait in seconds. 0 means no timeout.\r\n     *                            Defaults to 60-second timeout.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<Sandbox>} The forked Sandbox.\r\n     * @throws {DaytonaValidationError} - If timeout is a negative number\r\n     * @throws {DaytonaError} - If the fork operation fails or times out\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const sandbox = await daytona.get('my-sandbox');\r\n     * const forked = await sandbox._experimental_fork({ name: 'my-fork' });\r\n     * console.log(`Forked sandbox: ${forked.id}`);\r\n     */\r\n    _experimental_fork(params?: {\r\n        name?: string;\r\n    }, timeout?: number): Promise<Sandbox>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Creates a snapshot from the current state of the Sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * This captures the Sandbox's filesystem into a reusable snapshot that can be\r\n     * used to create new Sandboxes. The Sandbox will temporarily enter a\r\n     * 'snapshotting' state and return to its previous state when complete.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} name - Name for the new snapshot\r\n     * @param {number} [timeout] - Maximum time to wait in seconds. 0 means no timeout.\r\n     *                            Defaults to 60-second timeout.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     * @throws {DaytonaValidationError} - If timeout is a negative number\r\n     * @throws {DaytonaError} - If the snapshot operation fails or times out\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const sandbox = await daytona.get('my-sandbox');\r\n     * await sandbox._experimental_createSnapshot('my-snapshot');\r\n     * console.log('Snapshot created successfully');\r\n     */\r\n    _experimental_createSnapshot(name: string, timeout?: number): Promise<void>;\r\n    private waitForSnapshotComplete;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Deletes the Sandbox.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     */\r\n    delete(timeout?: number): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Waits for the Sandbox to reach the 'started' state.\r\n     *\r\n     * This method polls the Sandbox status until it reaches the 'started' state\r\n     * or encounters an error.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {number} [timeout] - Maximum time to wait in seconds. 0 means no timeout.\r\n     *                               Defaults to 60 seconds.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     * @throws {DaytonaError} - `DaytonaError` - If the sandbox ends up in an error state or fails to start within the timeout period.\r\n     */\r\n    waitUntilStarted(timeout?: number): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Wait for Sandbox to reach 'stopped' state.\r\n     *\r\n     * This method polls the Sandbox status until it reaches the 'stopped' state\r\n     * or encounters an error.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {number} [timeout] - Maximum time to wait in seconds. 0 means no timeout.\r\n     *                               Defaults to 60 seconds.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     * @throws {DaytonaError} - `DaytonaError` - If the sandbox fails to stop within the timeout period.\r\n     */\r\n    waitUntilStopped(timeout?: number): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Refreshes the Sandbox data from the API.\r\n     *\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * await sandbox.refreshData();\r\n     * console.log(`Sandbox ${sandbox.id}:`);\r\n     * console.log(`State: ${sandbox.state}`);\r\n     * console.log(`Resources: ${sandbox.cpu} CPU, ${sandbox.memory} GiB RAM`);\r\n     */\r\n    refreshData(): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Refreshes the sandbox activity to reset the timer for automated lifecycle management actions.\r\n     *\r\n     * This method updates the sandbox's last activity timestamp without changing its state.\r\n     * It is useful for keeping long-running sessions alive while there is still user activity.\r\n     *\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Keep sandbox activity alive\r\n     * await sandbox.refreshActivity();\r\n     */\r\n    refreshActivity(): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Set the auto-stop interval for the Sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * The Sandbox will automatically stop after being idle (no new events) for the specified interval.\r\n     * Events include any state changes or interactions with the Sandbox through the sdk.\r\n     * Interactions using Sandbox Previews are not included.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {number} interval - Number of minutes of inactivity before auto-stopping.\r\n     *                           Set to 0 to disable auto-stop. Default is 15 minutes.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     * @throws {DaytonaError} - `DaytonaError` - If interval is not a non-negative integer\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Auto-stop after 1 hour\r\n     * await sandbox.setAutostopInterval(60);\r\n     * // Or disable auto-stop\r\n     * await sandbox.setAutostopInterval(0);\r\n     */\r\n    setAutostopInterval(interval: number): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Set the auto-archive interval for the Sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * The Sandbox will automatically archive after being continuously stopped for the specified interval.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {number} interval - Number of minutes after which a continuously stopped Sandbox will be auto-archived.\r\n     *                           Set to 0 for the maximum interval. Default is 7 days.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     * @throws {DaytonaError} - `DaytonaError` - If interval is not a non-negative integer\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Auto-archive after 1 hour\r\n     * await sandbox.setAutoArchiveInterval(60);\r\n     * // Or use the maximum interval\r\n     * await sandbox.setAutoArchiveInterval(0);\r\n     */\r\n    setAutoArchiveInterval(interval: number): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Set the auto-delete interval for the Sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * The Sandbox will automatically delete after being continuously stopped for the specified interval.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {number} interval - Number of minutes after which a continuously stopped Sandbox will be auto-deleted.\r\n     *                           Set to negative value to disable auto-delete. Set to 0 to delete immediately upon stopping.\r\n     *                           By default, auto-delete is disabled.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Auto-delete after 1 hour\r\n     * await sandbox.setAutoDeleteInterval(60);\r\n     * // Or delete immediately upon stopping\r\n     * await sandbox.setAutoDeleteInterval(0);\r\n     * // Or disable auto-delete\r\n     * await sandbox.setAutoDeleteInterval(-1);\r\n     */\r\n    setAutoDeleteInterval(interval: number): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Updates outbound network policy for this sandbox on the runner (for example block all traffic,\r\n     * restore general internet access, or apply a CIDR allow list) without stopping the sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * This maps to the same mechanism as creating a sandbox with `networkBlockAll` / `networkAllowList`:\r\n     * the runner applies iptables rules to the sandbox container.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {UpdateSandboxNetworkSettings} settings - At least one of `networkBlockAll` or `networkAllowList` must be set.\r\n     *   Set `networkBlockAll` to `false` to restore outbound access after a block (and clear a stored allow list).\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Pause internet (outbound blocked)\r\n     * await sandbox.updateNetworkSettings({ networkBlockAll: true });\r\n     * // Resume internet\r\n     * await sandbox.updateNetworkSettings({ networkBlockAll: false });\r\n     */\r\n    updateNetworkSettings(settings: UpdateSandboxNetworkSettings): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Retrieves the preview link for the sandbox at the specified port. If the port is closed,\r\n     * it will be opened automatically. For private sandboxes, a token is included to grant access\r\n     * to the URL.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {number} port - The port to open the preview link on.\r\n     * @returns {PortPreviewUrl} The response object for the preview link, which includes the `url`\r\n     * and the `token` (to access private sandboxes).\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const previewLink = await sandbox.getPreviewLink(3000);\r\n     * console.log(`Preview URL: ${previewLink.url}`);\r\n     * console.log(`Token: ${previewLink.token}`);\r\n     */\r\n    getPreviewLink(port: number): Promise<PortPreviewUrl>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Retrieves a signed preview url for the sandbox at the specified port.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {number} port - The port to open the preview link on.\r\n     * @param {number} [expiresInSeconds] - The number of seconds the signed preview url will be valid for. Defaults to 60 seconds.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<SignedPortPreviewUrl>} The response object for the signed preview url.\r\n     */\r\n    getSignedPreviewUrl(port: number, expiresInSeconds?: number): Promise<SignedPortPreviewUrl>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Expires a signed preview url for the sandbox at the specified port.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {number} port - The port to expire the signed preview url on.\r\n     * @param {string} token - The token to expire the signed preview url on.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     */\r\n    expireSignedPreviewUrl(port: number, token: string): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Archives the sandbox, making it inactive and preserving its state. When sandboxes are archived, the entire filesystem\r\n     * state is moved to cost-effective object storage, making it possible to keep sandboxes available for an extended period.\r\n     * The tradeoff between archived and stopped states is that starting an archived sandbox takes more time, depending on its size.\r\n     * Sandbox must be stopped before archiving.\r\n     */\r\n    archive(): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Resizes the Sandbox resources.\r\n     *\r\n     * Changes the CPU, memory, or disk allocation for the Sandbox. Hot resize (on running\r\n     * sandbox) only allows CPU/memory increases. Disk resize requires a stopped sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {Resources} resources - New resource configuration. Only specified fields will be updated.\r\n     *   - cpu: Number of CPU cores (minimum: 1). For hot resize, can only be increased.\r\n     *   - memory: Memory in GiB (minimum: 1). For hot resize, can only be increased.\r\n     *   - disk: Disk space in GiB (can only be increased, requires stopped sandbox).\r\n     * @param {number} [timeout=60] - Timeout in seconds for the resize operation. 0 means no timeout.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     * @throws {DaytonaError} - If hot resize constraints are violated, disk resize attempted on running sandbox,\r\n     *   disk size decrease is attempted, no resource changes are specified, or resize operation times out.\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Increase CPU/memory on running sandbox (hot resize)\r\n     * await sandbox.resize({ cpu: 4, memory: 8 });\r\n     *\r\n     * // Change disk (sandbox must be stopped)\r\n     * await sandbox.stop();\r\n     * await sandbox.resize({ cpu: 2, memory: 4, disk: 30 });\r\n     */\r\n    resize(resources: Resources, timeout?: number): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Waits for the Sandbox resize operation to complete.\r\n     *\r\n     * This method polls the Sandbox status until the state is no longer 'resizing'.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {number} [timeout=60] - Maximum time to wait in seconds. 0 means no timeout.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     * @throws {DaytonaError} - If the sandbox ends up in an error state or resize times out.\r\n     */\r\n    waitForResizeComplete(timeout?: number): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Creates an SSH access token for the sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {number} expiresInMinutes - The number of minutes the SSH access token will be valid for.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<SshAccessDto>} The SSH access token.\r\n     */\r\n    createSshAccess(expiresInMinutes?: number): Promise<SshAccessDto>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Revokes an SSH access token for the sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} token - The token to revoke.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     */\r\n    revokeSshAccess(token: string): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Validates an SSH access token for the sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} token - The token to validate.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<SshAccessValidationDto>} The SSH access validation result.\r\n     */\r\n    validateSshAccess(token: string): Promise<SshAccessValidationDto>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Assigns the API sandbox data to the Sandbox object.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {SandboxDto} sandboxDto - The API sandbox instance to assign data from\r\n     * @returns {void}\r\n     */\r\n    private processSandboxDto;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Refreshes the Sandbox data from the API, but does not throw an error if the sandbox has been deleted.\r\n     * Instead, it sets the state to destroyed.\r\n     *\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     */\r\n    private refreshDataSafe;\r\n}\r\nexport interface ListSandboxesQuery {\r\n    /**\r\n     * Per-page fetch size. Does NOT limit the total number of Sandboxes returned.\r\n     * */\r\n    limit?: number;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Sort by field\r\n     * */\r\n    sort?: SandboxListSortField;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Sort direction\r\n     * */\r\n    order?: SandboxListSortDirection;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Filter by ID prefix (case-insensitive)\r\n     * */\r\n    id?: string;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Filter by name prefix (case-insensitive)\r\n     * */\r\n    name?: string;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Filter by labels\r\n     * */\r\n    labels?: Record<string, string>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Filter by states\r\n     * */\r\n    states?: SandboxState[];\r\n    /**\r\n     * Filter by snapshot names\r\n     * */\r\n    snapshots?: string[];\r\n    /**\r\n     * Filter by targets\r\n     * */\r\n    targets?: string[];\r\n    /**\r\n     * Filter by minimum CPU\r\n     * */\r\n    minCpu?: number;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Filter by maximum CPU\r\n     * */\r\n    maxCpu?: number;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Filter by minimum memory in GiB\r\n     * */\r\n    minMemoryGib?: number;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Filter by maximum memory in GiB\r\n     * */\r\n    maxMemoryGib?: number;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Filter by minimum disk space in GiB\r\n     * */\r\n    minDiskGib?: number;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Filter by maximum disk space in GiB\r\n     * */\r\n    maxDiskGib?: number;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Filter by public status\r\n     * */\r\n    isPublic?: boolean;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Filter by recoverable status\r\n     * */\r\n    isRecoverable?: boolean;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Include sandboxes created after this timestamp\r\n     * */\r\n    createdAtAfter?: Date;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Include sandboxes created before this timestamp\r\n     * */\r\n    createdAtBefore?: Date;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Include sandboxes with last activity after this timestamp\r\n     * */\r\n    lastActivityAfter?: Date;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Include sandboxes with last activity before this timestamp\r\n     * */\r\n    lastActivityBefore?: Date;\r\n}\r\n//# { Configuration, ProcessApi } from '@daytona/toolbox-api-client';\r\nimport type { Command, Session, SessionExecuteRequest, SessionExecuteResponse as ApiSessionExecuteResponse, PtySessionInfo } from '@daytona/toolbox-api-client';\r\nimport type { ExecuteResponse } from './types/ExecuteResponse';\r\nimport { PtyHandle } from './PtyHandle';\r\nimport type { PtyCreateOptions, PtyConnectOptions } from './types/Pty';\r\nexport declare const STDOUT_PREFIX_BYTES: Uint8Array<ArrayBuffer>;\r\nexport declare const STDERR_PREFIX_BYTES: Uint8Array<ArrayBuffer>;\r\nexport declare const MAX_PREFIX_LEN: number;\r\n/**\r\n * Parameters for code execution.\r\n */\r\nexport declare class CodeRunParams {\r\n    /**\r\n     * Command line arguments\r\n     */\r\n    argv?: string[];\r\n    /**\r\n     * Environment variables\r\n     */\r\n    env?: Record<string, string>;\r\n}\r\nexport interface SessionExecuteResponse extends ApiSessionExecuteResponse {\r\n    stdout?: string;\r\n    stderr?: string;\r\n}\r\nexport interface SessionCommandLogsResponse {\r\n    output?: string;\r\n    stdout?: string;\r\n    stderr?: string;\r\n}\r\n/**\r\n * Handles process and code execution within a Sandbox.\r\n *\r\n * @class\r\n */\r\nexport declare class Process {\r\n    private readonly clientConfig;\r\n    private readonly apiClient;\r\n    private readonly getPreviewToken;\r\n    private readonly language?;\r\n    constructor(clientConfig: Configuration, apiClient: ProcessApi, getPreviewToken: () => Promise<string>, language?: string);\r\n    /**\r\n     * Executes a shell command in the Sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} command - Shell command to execute\r\n     * @param {string} [cwd] - Working directory for command execution. If not specified, uses the sandbox working directory.\r\n     * @param {Record<string, string>} [env] - Environment variables to set for the command\r\n     * @param {number} [timeout] - Maximum time in seconds to wait for the command to complete.\r\n     * @returns {Promise<ExecuteResponse>} Command execution results containing:\r\n     *                                    - exitCode: The command's exit status\r\n     *                                    - result: Standard output from the command\r\n     *                                    - artifacts: ExecutionArtifacts object containing `stdout` (same as result) and `charts` (matplotlib charts metadata)\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Simple command\r\n     * const response = await process.executeCommand('echo \"Hello\"');\r\n     * console.log(response.artifacts.stdout);  // Prints: Hello\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Command with working directory\r\n     * const result = await process.executeCommand('ls', 'workspace/src');\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Command with timeout\r\n     * const result = await process.executeCommand('sleep 10', undefined, 5);\r\n     */\r\n    executeCommand(command: string, cwd?: string, env?: Record<string, string>, timeout?: number): Promise<ExecuteResponse>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Executes code in the Sandbox using the appropriate language runtime.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} code - Code to execute\r\n     * @param {CodeRunParams} params - Parameters for code execution\r\n     * @param {number} [timeout] - Maximum time in seconds to wait for execution to complete\r\n     * @returns {Promise<ExecuteResponse>} Code execution results containing:\r\n     *                                    - exitCode: The execution's exit status\r\n     *                                    - result: Standard output from the code\r\n     *                                    - artifacts: ExecutionArtifacts object containing `stdout` (same as result) and `charts` (matplotlib charts metadata)\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Run TypeScript code\r\n     * const response = await process.codeRun(`\r\n     *   const x = 10;\r\n     *   const y = 20;\r\n     *   console.log(\\`Sum: \\${x + y}\\`);\r\n     * `);\r\n     * console.log(response.artifacts.stdout);  // Prints: Sum: 30\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Run Python code with matplotlib\r\n     * const response = await process.codeRun(`\r\n     * import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\r\n     * import numpy as np\r\n     *\r\n     * x = np.linspace(0, 10, 30)\r\n     * y = np.sin(x)\r\n     *\r\n     * plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))\r\n     * plt.plot(x, y, 'b-', linewidth=2)\r\n     * plt.title('Line Chart')\r\n     * plt.xlabel('X-axis (seconds)')\r\n     * plt.ylabel('Y-axis (amplitude)')\r\n     * plt.grid(True)\r\n     * plt.show()\r\n     * `);\r\n     *\r\n     * if (response.artifacts?.charts) {\r\n     *   const chart = response.artifacts.charts[0];\r\n     *\r\n     *   console.log(`Type: ${chart.type}`);\r\n     *   console.log(`Title: ${chart.title}`);\r\n     *   if (chart.type === ChartType.LINE) {\r\n     *     const lineChart = chart as LineChart\r\n     *     console.log('X Label:', lineChart.x_label)\r\n     *     console.log('Y Label:', lineChart.y_label)\r\n     *     console.log('X Ticks:', lineChart.x_ticks)\r\n     *     console.log('Y Ticks:', lineChart.y_ticks)\r\n     *     console.log('X Tick Labels:', lineChart.x_tick_labels)\r\n     *     console.log('Y Tick Labels:', lineChart.y_tick_labels)\r\n     *     console.log('X Scale:', lineChart.x_scale)\r\n     *     console.log('Y Scale:', lineChart.y_scale)\r\n     *     console.log('Elements:')\r\n     *     console.dir(lineChart.elements, { depth: null })\r\n     *   }\r\n     * }\r\n     */\r\n    codeRun(code: string, params?: CodeRunParams, timeout?: number): Promise<ExecuteResponse>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Creates a new long-running background session in the Sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * Sessions are background processes that maintain state between commands, making them ideal for\r\n     * scenarios requiring multiple related commands or persistent environment setup. You can run\r\n     * long-running commands and monitor process status.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} sessionId - Unique identifier for the new session\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Create a new session\r\n     * const sessionId = 'my-session';\r\n     * await process.createSession(sessionId);\r\n     * const session = await process.getSession(sessionId);\r\n     * // Do work...\r\n     * await process.deleteSession(sessionId);\r\n     */\r\n    createSession(sessionId: string): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Get a session in the sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} sessionId - Unique identifier of the session to retrieve\r\n     * @returns {Promise<Session>} Session information including:\r\n     *                            - sessionId: The session's unique identifier\r\n     *                            - commands: List of commands executed in the session\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const session = await process.getSession('my-session');\r\n     * session.commands.forEach(cmd => {\r\n     *   console.log(`Command: ${cmd.command}`);\r\n     * });\r\n     */\r\n    getSession(sessionId: string): Promise<Session>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Get the sandbox entrypoint session\r\n     *\r\n     * @returns {Promise<Session>} Entrypoint session information including:\r\n     *                            - sessionId: The entrypoint session's unique identifier\r\n     *                            - commands: List of commands executed in the entrypoint session\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const session = await process.getEntrypointSession();\r\n     * session.commands.forEach(cmd => {\r\n     *   console.log(`Command: ${cmd.command}`);\r\n     * });\r\n     */\r\n    getEntrypointSession(): Promise<Session>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Gets information about a specific command executed in a session.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} sessionId - Unique identifier of the session\r\n     * @param {string} commandId - Unique identifier of the command\r\n     * @returns {Promise<Command>} Command information including:\r\n     *                            - id: The command's unique identifier\r\n     *                            - command: The executed command string\r\n     *                            - exitCode: Command's exit status (if completed)\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const cmd = await process.getSessionCommand('my-session', 'cmd-123');\r\n     * if (cmd.exitCode === 0) {\r\n     *   console.log(`Command ${cmd.command} completed successfully`);\r\n     * }\r\n     */\r\n    getSessionCommand(sessionId: string, commandId: string): Promise<Command>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Executes a command in an existing session.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} sessionId - Unique identifier of the session to use\r\n     * @param {SessionExecuteRequest} req - Command execution request containing:\r\n     *                                     - command: The command to execute\r\n     *                                     - runAsync: Whether to execute asynchronously\r\n     *                                     - suppressInputEcho: Whether to suppress input echo. Default is `false`.\r\n     * @param {number} timeout - Timeout in seconds\r\n     * @returns {Promise<SessionExecuteResponse>} Command execution results containing:\r\n     *                                           - cmdId: Unique identifier for the executed command\r\n     *                                           - output: Combined command output (stdout and stderr) (if synchronous execution)\r\n     *                                           - stdout: Standard output from the command\r\n     *                                           - stderr: Standard error from the command\r\n     *                                           - exitCode: Command exit status (if synchronous execution)\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Execute commands in sequence, maintaining state\r\n     * const sessionId = 'my-session';\r\n     *\r\n     * // Change directory\r\n     * await process.executeSessionCommand(sessionId, {\r\n     *   command: 'cd /home/daytona'\r\n     * });\r\n     *\r\n     * // Run command in new directory\r\n     * const result = await process.executeSessionCommand(sessionId, {\r\n     *   command: 'pwd'\r\n     * });\r\n     * console.log('[STDOUT]:', result.stdout);\r\n     * console.log('[STDERR]:', result.stderr);\r\n     */\r\n    executeSessionCommand(sessionId: string, req: SessionExecuteRequest, timeout?: number): Promise<SessionExecuteResponse>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Get the logs for a command executed in a session.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} sessionId - Unique identifier of the session\r\n     * @param {string} commandId - Unique identifier of the command\r\n     * @returns {Promise<SessionCommandLogsResponse>} Command logs containing: output (combined stdout and stderr), stdout and stderr\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const logs = await process.getSessionCommandLogs('my-session', 'cmd-123');\r\n     * console.log('[STDOUT]:', logs.stdout);\r\n     * console.log('[STDERR]:', logs.stderr);\r\n     */\r\n    getSessionCommandLogs(sessionId: string, commandId: string): Promise<SessionCommandLogsResponse>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Asynchronously retrieve and process the logs for a command executed in a session as they become available.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} sessionId - Unique identifier of the session\r\n     * @param {string} commandId - Unique identifier of the command\r\n     * @param {function} onStdout - Callback function to handle stdout log chunks\r\n     * @param {function} onStderr - Callback function to handle stderr log chunks\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const logs = await process.getSessionCommandLogs('my-session', 'cmd-123', (chunk) => {\r\n     *   console.log('[STDOUT]:', chunk);\r\n     * }, (chunk) => {\r\n     *   console.log('[STDERR]:', chunk);\r\n     * });\r\n     */\r\n    getSessionCommandLogs(sessionId: string, commandId: string, onStdout: (chunk: string) => void, onStderr: (chunk: string) => void): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Get the logs for the sandbox entrypoint session.\r\n     *\r\n     * @returns {Promise<SessionCommandLogsResponse>} Command logs containing: output (combined stdout and stderr), stdout and stderr\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const logs = await process.getEntrypointLogs();\r\n     * console.log('[STDOUT]:', logs.stdout);\r\n     * console.log('[STDERR]:', logs.stderr);\r\n     */\r\n    getEntrypointLogs(): Promise<SessionCommandLogsResponse>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Asynchronously retrieve and process the logs for the entrypoint session as they become available.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {function} onStdout - Callback function to handle stdout log chunks\r\n     * @param {function} onStderr - Callback function to handle stderr log chunks\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const logs = await process.getEntrypointLogs((chunk) => {\r\n     *   console.log('[STDOUT]:', chunk);\r\n     * }, (chunk) => {\r\n     *   console.log('[STDERR]:', chunk);\r\n     * });\r\n     */\r\n    getEntrypointLogs(onStdout: (chunk: string) => void, onStderr: (chunk: string) => void): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Sends input data to a command executed in a session.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} sessionId - Unique identifier of the session\r\n     * @param {string} commandId - Unique identifier of the command\r\n     * @param {string} data - Input data to send\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     */\r\n    sendSessionCommandInput(sessionId: string, commandId: string, data: string): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Lists all active sessions in the Sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * @returns {Promise<Session[]>} Array of active sessions\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * const sessions = await process.listSessions();\r\n     * sessions.forEach(session => {\r\n     *   console.log(`Session ${session.sessionId}:`);\r\n     *   session.commands.forEach(cmd => {\r\n     *     console.log(`- ${cmd.command} (${cmd.exitCode})`);\r\n     *   });\r\n     * });\r\n     */\r\n    listSessions(): Promise<Session[]>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Delete a session from the Sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} sessionId - Unique identifier of the session to delete\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Clean up a completed session\r\n     * await process.deleteSession('my-session');\r\n     */\r\n    deleteSession(sessionId: string): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Create a new PTY (pseudo-terminal) session in the sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * Creates an interactive terminal session that can execute commands and handle user input.\r\n     * The PTY session behaves like a real terminal, supporting features like command history.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {PtyCreateOptions & PtyConnectOptions} options - PTY session configuration including creation and connection options\r\n     * @returns {Promise<PtyHandle>} PTY handle for managing the session\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Create a PTY session with custom configuration\r\n     * const ptyHandle = await process.createPty({\r\n     *   id: 'my-interactive-session',\r\n     *   cwd: '/workspace',\r\n     *   envs: { TERM: 'xterm-256color', LANG: 'en_US.UTF-8' },\r\n     *   cols: 120,\r\n     *   rows: 30,\r\n     *   onData: (data) => {\r\n     *     // Handle terminal output\r\n     *     const text = new TextDecoder().decode(data);\r\n     *     process.stdout.write(text);\r\n     *   },\r\n     * });\r\n     *\r\n     * // Wait for connection to be established\r\n     * await ptyHandle.waitForConnection();\r\n     *\r\n     * // Send commands to the terminal\r\n     * await ptyHandle.sendInput('ls -la\\n');\r\n     * await ptyHandle.sendInput('echo \"Hello, PTY!\"\\n');\r\n     * await ptyHandle.sendInput('exit\\n');\r\n     *\r\n     * // Wait for completion and get result\r\n     * const result = await ptyHandle.wait();\r\n     * console.log(`PTY session completed with exit code: ${result.exitCode}`);\r\n     *\r\n     * // Clean up\r\n     * await ptyHandle.disconnect();\r\n     */\r\n    createPty(options?: PtyCreateOptions & PtyConnectOptions): Promise<PtyHandle>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Connect to an existing PTY session in the sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * Establishes a WebSocket connection to an existing PTY session, allowing you to\r\n     * interact with a previously created terminal session.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} sessionId - ID of the PTY session to connect to\r\n     * @param {PtyConnectOptions} options - Options for the connection including data handler\r\n     * @returns {Promise<PtyHandle>} PTY handle for managing the session\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Connect to an existing PTY session\r\n     * const handle = await process.connectPty('my-session', {\r\n     *   onData: (data) => {\r\n     *     // Handle terminal output\r\n     *     const text = new TextDecoder().decode(data);\r\n     *     process.stdout.write(text);\r\n     *   },\r\n     * });\r\n     *\r\n     * // Wait for connection to be established\r\n     * await handle.waitForConnection();\r\n     *\r\n     * // Send commands to the existing session\r\n     * await handle.sendInput('pwd\\n');\r\n     * await handle.sendInput('ls -la\\n');\r\n     * await handle.sendInput('exit\\n');\r\n     *\r\n     * // Wait for completion\r\n     * const result = await handle.wait();\r\n     * console.log(`Session exited with code: ${result.exitCode}`);\r\n     *\r\n     * // Clean up\r\n     * await handle.disconnect();\r\n     */\r\n    connectPty(sessionId: string, options?: PtyConnectOptions): Promise<PtyHandle>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * List all PTY sessions in the sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * Retrieves information about all PTY sessions, both active and inactive,\r\n     * that have been created in this sandbox.\r\n     *\r\n     * @returns {Promise<PtySessionInfo[]>} Array of PTY session information\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // List all PTY sessions\r\n     * const sessions = await process.listPtySessions();\r\n     *\r\n     * for (const session of sessions) {\r\n     *   console.log(`Session ID: ${session.id}`);\r\n     *   console.log(`Active: ${session.active}`);\r\n     *   console.log(`Created: ${session.createdAt}`);\r\n     *   }\r\n     *   console.log('---');\r\n     * }\r\n     */\r\n    listPtySessions(): Promise<PtySessionInfo[]>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Get detailed information about a specific PTY session.\r\n     *\r\n     * Retrieves comprehensive information about a PTY session including its current state,\r\n     * configuration, and metadata.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} sessionId - ID of the PTY session to retrieve information for\r\n     * @returns {Promise<PtySessionInfo>} PTY session information\r\n     *\r\n     * @throws {Error} If the PTY session doesn't exist\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Get details about a specific PTY session\r\n     * const session = await process.getPtySessionInfo('my-session');\r\n     *\r\n     * console.log(`Session ID: ${session.id}`);\r\n     * console.log(`Active: ${session.active}`);\r\n     * console.log(`Working Directory: ${session.cwd}`);\r\n     * console.log(`Terminal Size: ${session.cols}x${session.rows}`);\r\n     *\r\n     * if (session.processId) {\r\n     *   console.log(`Process ID: ${session.processId}`);\r\n     * }\r\n     */\r\n    getPtySessionInfo(sessionId: string): Promise<PtySessionInfo>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Kill a PTY session and terminate its associated process.\r\n     *\r\n     * Forcefully terminates the PTY session and cleans up all associated resources.\r\n     * This will close any active connections and kill the underlying shell process.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} sessionId - ID of the PTY session to kill\r\n     * @returns {Promise<void>}\r\n     *\r\n     * @throws {Error} If the PTY session doesn't exist or cannot be killed\r\n     *\r\n     * @note This operation is irreversible. Any unsaved work in the terminal session will be lost.\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Kill a specific PTY session\r\n     * await process.killPtySession('my-session');\r\n     *\r\n     * // Verify the session is no longer active\r\n     * try {\r\n     *   const info = await process.getPtySessionInfo('my-session');\r\n     *   console.log(`Session still exists but active: ${info.active}`);\r\n     * } catch (error) {\r\n     *   console.log('Session has been completely removed');\r\n     * }\r\n     */\r\n    killPtySession(sessionId: string): Promise<void>;\r\n    /**\r\n     * Resize a PTY session's terminal dimensions.\r\n     *\r\n     * Changes the terminal size of an active PTY session. This is useful when the\r\n     * client terminal is resized or when you need to adjust the display for different\r\n     * output requirements.\r\n     *\r\n     * @param {string} sessionId - ID of the PTY session to resize\r\n     * @param {number} cols - New number of terminal columns\r\n     * @param {number} rows - New number of terminal rows\r\n     * @returns {Promise<PtySessionInfo>} Updated session information reflecting the new terminal size\r\n     *\r\n     * @throws {Error} If the PTY session doesn't exist or resize operation fails\r\n     *\r\n     * @note The resize operation will send a SIGWINCH signal to the shell process,\r\n     * allowing terminal applications to adapt to the new size.\r\n     *\r\n     * @example\r\n     * // Resize a PTY session to a larger terminal\r\n     * const updatedInfo = await process.resizePtySession('my-session', 150, 40);\r\n     * console.log(`Terminal resized to ${updatedInfo.cols}x${updatedInfo.rows}`);\r\n     *\r\n     * // You can also use the PtyHandle's resize method\r\n     * await ptyHandle.resize(150, 40); // cols, rows\r\n     */\r\n    resizePtySession(sessionId: string, cols: number, rows: number): Promise<PtySessionInfo>;\r\n}\r\n//# sourceMappingURL=Process.d.ts.map","isTruncated":false}},"providerOptions":{"vertex":{"thoughtSignature":"AY89a1+mo0EBmJGZXnhpeHsfsZ6AbQk9ESRko1PrUfaan665WFXP5aqDXiGiEtcHqBccTrvwKPK2D4N8NUEnJol/rXwYejsgHWTHg+eu9nP+5rblGaWOVhIk+WfmcHZC0n84hPnEnG7QG9RNOyUk9KYnnDbVD4yPd4vQoQCJ6uQ0PNXJ"}}}]},{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"reasoning","text":"**Executing Commands**\n\nI've confirmed that `process.executeCommand` is the appropriate function to use here. not set');\n  }\n\n  const sandboxName = `exec-ts-${runId}`;\n  const logFilePath = path.join(__dirname, 'output.log');\n  \n  const daytona = new Daytona();\n  let sandbox;\n  try {\n    // Create
